We're more willing to be a little tolerant with the foibles and mistakes of people that we are committed to, married to, in this daily, ongoing relationship than we are with vendor in the same category with whom we are not very involved. And so it gives you a little room for flaws that now aren't fatal, that might be fatal otherwise. So this is what you call your top of concept? I mean, Disney every so often aggravates me. But to disconnect, I mean, I got a moment to dismantle the house. And I got to change my whole life. And I refer people there all the time. So the emotional disconnect from all that. Whereas my dry cleaner, they screw up once. Yeah, there's another one right next door, right? Yeah, absolutely. So what you're saying basically is you want to be on the top of people's consciousness. I want to move in. I mean, I want to move in. I want to move in physically. I want to take up some space every day. And I want to take up space both physically and mentally. And mentally. Yeah, right. And I want to be part of their lives. And somebody running a shoe store can do this. Absolutely. You just have to not talk about shoes all the time. Right, yeah, absolutely. And so then the example I always give, the person at a cocktail party, and we just keep using the dentist example, but that's fine. Or we can use the shoe guy example. And they're saying, hey, I'm going to go buy my kids some shoes. And you say, well, you should. Our guy. Our guy. I got the best shoe guy on the planet. Yeah, you don't need to stand around. So salespeople, franchise owners. There's a tendency to great idea, but it's somebody else's job. Right, exactly. Right. Well, but it's your income. Right. You know? I mean, just because your employer is stupid doesn't mean you have to be stupid. Right, yeah, exactly. So you've given tons of actionable strategies. Where should somebody start with this? To start building. So obviously they've got to go through this again. Okay. So salesperson, you've got to go through it again. Read the transcript all that. I mean, you can take small baby steps if you wish. There is the Renegade Millionaire Principle simultaneous, not sequential. So the truth is you've got to create a foundation. So A, you've got to make sure that you've got the minimum and he's right. You know, do I really have happy customers? You know? I mean, is this thing really working? Right. It's my customers that are sort of tolerating me because it's more trouble to, you know, so Time Warner doesn't get a lot of referrals. Okay? If you have it to be stuck with Time Warner Cable, you're stuck with them. So I got them. I hate them. Right? Now the idea of switching to Direct TV and not knowing is it going to be better or worse, so... You should switch. It's better. I'm at Time Warner, you know? Yeah. But it's not like I'm running around telling anybody, you know, man, you should sign up for Time Warner. Right? Right. And you're the first person to actually tell me I should sign up for Direct TV. So you kind of got to make sure you got the minimum and he in place, you know? And then you got to think about the culture around it. How do we put in place things that can communicate to people that this is a referral driven business, that that's what we do around here? Then you can start putting pieces. So newsletter first, right? And there are people in our world like Sean Buck who do a phenomenal job with that being 90% done for you and not totally off the shelf and not being, you know, and being good. I will always say you can't be doing your own. But if you're not going to do your own, the next best thing is somebody who really knows what they're doing who will do it for you. So you put the newsletter in place. If you... That's easy too. I mean, that's... If you can't do a report of the month, at least get the general static standard information piece people can drive to in place. The one thing that represents best what you do or what you want to sell most. Right? And then get one every three months. And then...
